name: prompt-string-form
note: The legacy completions API carries the user text in a `prompt` string rather than a `messages` list. This checks the router uses that string as the text it routes on: 'escalate' is present, and this policy sends requests containing 'escalate' to cloud.

name: prompt-array-form
note: A `prompt` may also be a list of strings, which the router should join into one text. This policy routes to cloud only when the text holds both 'alpha' and 'omega' — one is in the first list item, the other in the second — so reaching cloud confirms every item was included (reading only the first item would drop 'omega' and it would stay local).

name: prompt-array-skips-non-string-parts
note: A `prompt` list can contain non-text items (here an object); the router should ignore those and use only the strings. The object holds the word 'escalate', which this policy would route to cloud under a different rule if the object were turned into text. Because the request instead matches on 'alpha' + 'omega' (the two string items), the object was ignored, not read.

name: input-string-form
note: The Responses API can carry the user text as a plain `input` string. This checks the router uses that string as the text it routes on: 'escalate' is present, so it routes to cloud.

name: input-array-role-tagged
note: An `input` may be a list of role-tagged messages; the router routes on the last user message's text. Here that text is an 'input_text' part (the Responses API spelling of a text part, handled like chat's 'text'), so 'escalate' routes to cloud.

name: input-roleless-fallback
note: When an `input` list has no role-tagged user message, the router falls back to concatenating every plain-string item into one text. Both 'alpha' (first item) and 'omega' (second) must appear to reach cloud, so routing there confirms every item was concatenated — otherwise it would stay local.
